verification_gate: Red tests precede implementation; bounded .nu, .rs, and .toml fixtures round-trip exact bytes, paths, permissions, AST metadata, and hashes through the native MessagePack plugin; duplicate content is content-addressed once; raw benchmark samples qualify all performance wording.

gate_result.red_commit: 8dfe9bd (nu_plugin archbp-001-ingest-envelope): e2e + 9 unit tests, 0 passed / 9 failed on stubs
gate_result.dedup_evidence: e2e dedup_hit_count=1 unique_blob_count=5 over 6 fixtures incl. byte-identical dup pair; unit idempotency re-ingest dedup_hit_count=3 unique_blob_count=0
gate_result.blake3_dedup_index: codedb-store-redb blake3_index table, lazily created, blake3->sha256
gate_result.benchmark_artifact: nu_plugin logs/ingestion-benchmark/samples.json (30 raw wall-clock samples, median ~47ms full typed round trip including nu startup and plugin registration)
gate_result.workspace_tests: cargo test --workspace: 276 passed, 0 failed; nushell syntax gate green; truth_surface --check-source 815 files ok
gate_result.blocked_paths_honored: no envctl ownership touched; no PostgreSQL mutation (redb-only lane; authoritative PG commits remain envctl-owned per R10); no generated Yazelix runtime edits; no secrets

proof_summary: ARCHBP-001 built the bounded native-Nushell ingestion pipeline red-first in the nu_plugin repository (branch archbp-001-ingest-envelope, PR FlexNetOS/nu_plugin#41). The typed `codedb ingest-envelope` plugin command takes a record pipeline input over the native MessagePack protocol, validates fail-closed (exact schema version, bounded counts and sizes, clean relative paths, sha256 and blake3 recomputed over decoded bytes), and persists through the redb store where identical bytes are content-addressed exactly once via a BLAKE3 index; `codedb ingest-report` and the existing materialization path round-trip exact bytes, relative paths, unix modes, module paths, hashes, and flattened Nushell AST rows for the committed .nu/.rs/.toml fixtures end-to-end. Thirty raw wall-clock samples qualify all performance wording; no zero-copy or vectorization claim is made.
